subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Load a model file and print basic statistics. Full postprocessing is applied unless the `-r` switch is specified

options.description: No postprocessing, do a raw import
Print verbose info such as node transform data
Print only minimal info

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export a file to one of the supported output formats

options.description: Export file formats supported by Assimp

args.name: model
args.description: Relative or absolute path to the input model

args.name: out
args.description: Relative or absolute path to write the output export to

options.description: Zero-based index of the texture to be extracted
Specifies whether output BMPs contain an alpha channel or not
Specifies the output file format. Supported formats are BMP and TGA. The default value is BMP (if a full output filename is specified, the output file format is taken from its extension)
Prevents the tool from adding the _img<n> suffix to all filenames. This option must be specified together with -t to ensure that just one image is written
Fast post processing preset, runs just a few important steps
Default post processing: runs all recommended steps
Fires almost all post processing steps

args.name: out
args.description: Relative or absolute path to write the output images to

subcommands.name: dump
subcommands.description: Convert models to a binary or textual dump (ASSBIN/ASSXML)

options.description: If true, the dump is written in binary format
If true, the dump is shortened to include only min/max values for all vertex components and animation channels
If true, Compressed
Fast post processing preset, runs just a few important steps
Default post processing: runs all recommended steps
Fires almost all post processing steps
